LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAY-07-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DAVID FANTOM'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. OFF CALENDAR. THE DECLARATION OF JAIME COUCHE IN SUPPORT OF THE OPPOSITION SHOWS THAT THE PARTIES FAILED TO MEET AND CONFER ABOUT THE FAC'S SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EMBEZZLEMENT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

DISCOVERY 302, MAY-16-2024 HEARING ON DEFENDANT'S REVISED AND RE-NOTICE OF MOTION & MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS (SET ONE). COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT DOES NOT STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE MATTER IS DISCUSSED. AT CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSION, THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO LAW & MOTION ON JUN-03-2024 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 302. THE JUDGE PRO TEM IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT A REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION. JUDGE PRO TEM: WILLIAM LYNN. CLERK: R. WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DAVID FANTOM's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant David Fantom's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled in part and sustained in part with leave to amend.

The FAC alleges conversion (count 1) adequately for the pleading stage. Fantom is one of two named people defined as "Defendants" (FAC 2:8-9); references to Defendants include him. Moreover, a plaintiff need not "establish" or "show" anything at the pleading stage (see id. at 11:6, 12:3); on demurrer, all allegations are taken as true. Demurrer overruled.

The FAC's fraud and fraud-based claims of embezzlement and RICO (counts 2, 3 and 9) are not pled with specificity: "how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Demurrer sustained with leave to amend.

The disfavored uncertainty demurrer is overruled.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DAVID FANTOM's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant David Fantom's motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint (FAC) is denied. The FAC adequately pleads bases for punitive damages against Fantom.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
